may eight
A Letter for your Birthday

Esther

The Loveliest Day Of The Year
“If I could plan a surprise the size of how much you mean to me,
it would still feel small. So I built you a few quiet pages instead
meant only for you.”
happy birthday, my love
scroll, gently
i. A Wish

For The Year Ahead

my dearest girl,

may this year wrap around you like the softest morning light. may you laugh until your cheeks ache and your stomach hurts. may every door you knock on, open and may the rooms behind them be kind.

may your work mean something. may your rest be deep. may your phone bring more good news than bad. may old friends find you and new ones surprise you.

and most of all may you always know, deeply and certainly and completely, how loved you are. by me. by everyone lucky enough to know you. by the small, ordinary universe that arranged itself around the day you were born.

— this is just the beginning.
ii. A Letter

To You, On Your Day

Sealed
with
love
My dearest Esther,

You didn’t see this coming, did you? good. that was the whole point. Because someone like you, someone who lights up rooms without ever realising she’s doing it deserves to be caught off guard, just once, by how loved she truly is.

You are the rare kind of wonderful. The kind that doesn’t announce itself. The kind that shows up in small, steady ways in the way you laugh mid-sentence as if joy can’t wait its turn, in the way you carry yourself through the heavy days, in the way you make ordinary moments feel like keepsakes someone will want to remember.

Watching you become more you over time has been one of the quiet privileges of my life. Softer where it matters, braver where it counts, sweeter than the world deserves but exactly as sweet as I needed. You have this way of making everything around you feel a little kinder, a little gentler as if your presence alone tilts the day toward something good.

And so today is yours. Every page of it. Every minute. Every quiet, unhurried thought. I just wanted you to get up and find hidden in the corners of an ordinary morning proof that someone sees you. Really sees you. Not the version you hold up for the world. All of you. Even the parts you think nobody notices. Especially those.

I love you in the small way of remembering how detailed your cooking and the need for me to review appropriately, and in the large way of meaning it when I say forever. I love you in a way that means I would build a hundred little surprises like this one every year, in a hundred quiet ways just to keep watching the look on your face when you realise.

Happy birthday, my love. You are wonderful beyond words. And somehow, always, mine.

always and entirely yours,
— Dominion
iii. A Poem
For Esther
A small verse · with all my heart
There is a softness in the way you move
through ordinary mornings, ordinary rooms
a quiet kind of brilliance, the sort that doesn’t ask
to be seen, and so the world leans in to look.
You are every good thing I keep finding twice:
in the gold of late afternoons,
in the small, generous chemistry of your delicious food,
in the laughter that escapes you mid-sentence
like it could not bear to wait.
Today I am holding all of it up to the light
every photograph, every memory, every tomorrow
and saying, simply: here she is.
The most wonderful person I know.
And, somehow,always mine.
happy birthday, my love.
iv. A Gallery

Pieces Of You

a small museum of moments, every photograph here is a tiny argument for how lucky the world is to have you in it.

~ fin ~
Today, and every day after,
I choose you.
thank you for letting me be the person who gets to celebrate you loudly and quietly, in person and in pages, in the morning light and long after the candles are blown out. i hope today feels exactly like you deserve. and tomorrow. and the next one. and forever.
made for esther with all my love
